title: pasania induta
intro: Pasania induta, also known as Indian beech or pasania, is a species of evergreen tree native to the Himalayas. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Pasania induta include its ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Herbal preparations of Pasania induta include teas, decoctions, and infusions, which can be made by boiling the leaves, bark, or roots of the plant in water.
